rag_rules: Fix AGREE diagnostics by aligning helper types with expressions and using only visible identifiers from the current component scope.
When AGREE reports type mismatches, ensure declarations match the actual AADL data types or remove invalid clauses.
If diagnostics reference implementation-level elements in a component type annex, move valid bindings to the implementation scope or delete invalid guarantees.
generic_rules: Validate AGREE expressions for type consistency and avoid introducing undefined or mismatched identifiers.
Ensure all AGREE constants, equations, and guarantees reference only visible features or helpers declared in the same annex.
When repairing AGREE contracts, preserve existing architecture and avoid adding new component-level declarations.
Use only AADL-defined data types and identifiers; do not introduce package-qualified references without matching imports.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid annex scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Helper declarations must specify correct scalar types: int, real, bool; avoid undefined or aliased types.
Guarantee expressions must resolve to valid types; avoid Boolean comparisons on opaque or non-numeric data.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new identifiers or types that are not visible in the current AADL component scope.
Do not attempt to fix unresolved references by inventing new declarations; instead, remove or rewrite clauses.
Do not mix implementation-level references in component type annexes unless they are valid outputs or assignments.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE validator errors about duplicate element names in annexes
Model Analyst or Requirement Analyst tasks
